contact.title: Reach us.
contact.lead: For an AOG case, WhatsApp is fastest. For RFQs, the supply wizard captures everything we need. For everything else, email works.
contact.flAddressNote: Florida-based, serving every US port 24/7. Exact service address shared on request.
contact.tagline: Worldwide service available at all US ports — 24/7
contact.coverageH3: Service available at all US ports.
contact.coverageLead: Florida operations · Wyoming LLC · 24/7 worldwide. We dispatch to any US port within hours and ship AOG spares the same day.
contact.fastestPathsLead: For RFQs and service requests, the forms below capture vessel, port, urgency and technical context so we can quote the same day. Pure email is fine too.

about.lead: Levent Marine is a commercial-vessel-focused marine electrical service desk and technical parts supplier. Founded and operated by a working STCW Reg. III/6 Electro-Technical Officer and control-system engineer — twelve years at sea as an ETO across bulk carriers, container ships, cement carriers and offshore research vessels, plus two years ashore as an electrical superintendent. We dispatch an engineer to any US port within hours, ship AOG-grade spares the same day, and deliver class-aware reports the superintendent can hand straight to the surveyor.
about.backgroundH2: On the deckplate, not behind a desk.
about.backgroundP1: The engineer behind Levent Marine is a control-system engineer holding a current STCW III/6 ETO licence with HV ≤ 1000 V endorsement — twelve years sailing as an ETO on bulk carriers, container ships, cement carriers and offshore research vessels, then two years ashore as an electrical superintendent. Credentials include Advanced Fire Fighting (VI/3), Medical First Aid (VI/4-1), Basic Safety (VI/1) and Gas / Oil & Chemical Tanker familiarisation, plus a US TWIC card, OSHA and NFPA 70E electrical-safety certification. Every RFQ, every dispatch and every report is reviewed by someone who has actually terminated a busbar on a moving deck — not forwarded to a generic procurement agent.
about.backgroundP2: When the OEM is obsolete, we propose a compatible replacement and document where it matches and where it differs. The final call belongs to your superintendent — we provide the paper trail and the class wording.
about.notDo1: No yachts, no pleasure craft, no superyacht refits.
about.notDo2: No public pricing — every order is RFQ-first to protect margin and audit trail.
about.notDo3: No customer login required — single sender / sole contact through the wizard.
about.notDo4: No subcontracting of class-witnessed work to unbranded third parties.
about.certsH2: Certifications held — STCW endorsements plus US safety credentials.
about.equipmentLead: We arrive with the meter, not just the wrench. Standard go-bag includes insulation testers up to 5 kV, infrared cameras, power-quality analysers and protection-relay test sets. Heavier kit (primary injection, partial-discharge, transformer ratiometer) follows on the next freight if the job calls for it.
about.clientsH2: A small list, deliberately. Long-running working relationships.
about.classH2: Reports written for the eight major class societies.
about.classLead: Every dispatch concludes with a report formatted to the requesting class society's template — including measured values, calibration certificate IDs, photographic evidence and a recommended action list with timing. Hand it straight to the surveyor; no rewriting required.
about.coverageLead: Service available at all US ports — East Coast, West Coast, Gulf, Great Lakes, plus Anchorage and Honolulu. Worldwide attendance available on request.
about.nextStepH2: Vessel due in a US port? Tell us the system and the ETA.
about.nextStepLead: Three taps in the wizard puts an engineer or a quote on your superintendent's desk. Email + WhatsApp acknowledgement within minutes — first available technician on the phone within an hour.

portsIndex.h1: Marine electrical service at every major US port.
portsIndex.lead: Florida-based operations, Wyoming LLC. We dispatch an engineer to your vessel within hours at any US East Coast, Gulf, West Coast or Pacific port. AOG-grade spares ship the same day. Pick the port — port-specific intake notes, local freight gateways and likely fault patterns wait inside.
portsIndex.notListedLead: Worldwide attendance available on request. Tell us the port, the system and the ETA — we route from the nearest US gateway and ship parts on the same routing.
